debug_info.goal:
μ ν : Measure ℝ
inst✝¹ : IsProbabilityMeasure μ
inst✝ : IsProbabilityMeasure ν
n : ℕ
i j : Fin n
h : i < j
⊢ HasLaw (fun ω => wignerMatrixMap n ω i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n)

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
classical
  have h₁ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
  -- We need to show that the distribution of (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j follows μ.
  -- By definition, for i < j, (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j = ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩, and the measure on ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩ is μ.
  have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), (le_of_lt h)⟩) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    (try {
      split_ifs <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;>
      (try { contradiction })
      <;>
      (try { linarith })
      <;>
      (try { aesop })
    })
    <;>
    (try {
      aesop
    })
  rw [h₂]
  -- Since the function is the projection of ω onto ⟨(i, j), _⟩, and the measure at that point is μ, the law is μ.
  have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₄ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (fun p ↦ if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ) := by
      rfl
    rw [h₄]
    -- Using the fact that the projections of the product measure are independent and follow the respective measures.
    have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩) (if (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩ : {p : Fin n × Fin n // p.1 ≤ p.2}).val.1 = (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩ : {p : Fin n × Fin n // p.1 ≤ p.2}).val.2 then ν else μ) (Measure.pi (fun p ↦ if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ)) := by
      apply hasLaw_fst (by
        -- Show that the type {p : Fin n × Fin n // p.1 ≤ p.2} is finite, hence the product measure is a product of probabilities.
        infer_instance)
      <;>
      simp [Finset.mem_univ]
    have h₆ : (if (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩ : {p : Fin n × Fin n // p.1 ≤ p.2}).val.1 = (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩ : {p : Fin n × Fin n // p.1 ≤ p.2}).val.2 then ν else μ) = μ := by
      dsimp only
      have h₇ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
      simp [Fin.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff] at h ⊢ <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { split_ifs <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff] <;> omega }) <;>
      (try { aesop })
    rw [h₆] at h₅
    exact h₅
  exact h₃
